        parseYAML
      • parseYAML(input: YAML): YAMLObject
      • parseYAML[T: object | ref object](parser: var YamlParser; v: var T)
      • parseYAML[T](input: YAML; t: typedesc[T]): T
        put
      • put(obj: YAMLObject; key: string; value: YamlNode)

This module provides a YAML parser and serializer for Nim.

It can convert Nim objects, tables and arrays to YAML format and parse YAML strings into Nim data structures.

If your Nim data structures contains doc-comments, they will be included as comments in the generated YAML output. This allows you to write self-documenting Nim code that can be easily converted to YAML configuration files

Imports

private/types, json

Types

OpenParserYamlError = object of CatchableError
Exception type for errors encountered during YAML parsing or dumping 
YAML = string
A simple alias for YAML strings 
YamlLexer = object
Performs lexical analysis on a YAML input string, producing tokens for the parser 
YamlNode {.acyclic.} = ref object
  case kind*: YamlValueKind
  of yamlInteger:
    intValue*: int64         ## Represents an integer value in YAML
  of yamlFloat:
    floatValue*: float64     ## Represents a floating-point value in YAML
  of yamlString:
    strValue*: string        ## Represents a string value in YAML
  of yamlBoolean:
    boolValue*: bool         ## Represents a boolean value in YAML
  of yamlObject:
    objValue*: OrderedTableRef[string, YamlNode] ## Represents a YAML mapping (object) with string keys and YamlNode values
  of yamlArray:
    arrValue*: seq[YamlNode] ## Represents a YAML sequence (array) of YamlNode items
  of yamlNull:
    nil
Represents a node in the YAML data structure, which can be a scalar, object or array 
YAMLObject = OrderedTableRef[string, YamlNode]
Represents a simple 
YamlParser = object
Parses a sequence of tokens from the YamlLexer to build a YAMLObject 
YamlToken = ref object
  kind*: YamlTokenKind
  value*: string
  line*: int
  col*: int
  pos*: int
  wsno*: int
  indent*: int
Represents a lexical token produced by the YAML lexer 
YamlTokenKind = enum
  ytkEOF = "EOF", ytkIdentifier = "Identifier", ytkColon = ":", ytkComma = ",",
  ytkDash = "-", ytkLB = "[", ytkRB = "]", ytkLC = "{", ytkRC = "}",
  ytkPipe = "|", ytkGT = ">", ytkQuote = "\"", ytkSingleQuote = "\'", ytkString,
  ytkFloat, ytkInteger, ytkBoolean, ytkComment, ytkUnknown
YamlValueKind = enum
  yamlInteger, yamlFloat, yamlString, yamlBoolean, yamlObject, yamlArray,
  yamlNull

Procs

proc `$`(yamlObject: YAMLObject): string {....raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
Return a JSON string representation of the YAMLObject 
proc dumpHook(s: var string; v: YamlNode) {....raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
proc get(n: YamlNode; key: string): YamlNode {....raises: [KeyError], tags: [],
    forbids: [].}
Recursively access nested YAML data using dot-separated keys. Example: get(config, "user.name") 
proc get(obj: YAMLObject; key: string): YamlNode {....raises: [KeyError], tags: [],
    forbids: [].}
Access a value from a YAMLObject using a key 
proc getArray(n: YamlNode): seq[YamlNode] {....raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
Get array value or empty seq if not an array node 
proc getBool(n: YamlNode): bool {....raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
Get boolean value or false if not a boolean node 
proc getFloat(n: YamlNode): float64 {....raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
Get float value or 0.0 if not a float node 
proc getInt(n: YamlNode): int64 {....raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
Get integer value or 0 if not an integer node 
proc getObject(n: YamlNode): OrderedTableRef[string, YamlNode] {....raises: [],
    tags: [], forbids: [].}
Get object value or empty table if not an object node 
proc getStr(n: YamlNode): string {....raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
Get string value or "" if not a string node 
proc getValue(v: YamlNode): string {....raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
Get the string representation of a YamlNode value (for debugging) 
proc newYamlArray(): YamlNode {....raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
Create a new YamlNode of kind yamlArray 
proc newYamlBoolean(b: bool): YamlNode {....raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
Create a new YamlNode of kind yamlBoolean 
proc newYamlFloat(f: float64): YamlNode {....raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
Create a new YamlNode of kind yamlFloat 
proc newYamlInteger(i: int64): YamlNode {....raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
Create a new YamlNode of kind yamlInteger 
proc newYamlLexer(input: string): YamlLexer {....raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
Create a new YamlLexer for the given input string 
proc newYamlNull(): YamlNode {....raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
Create a new YamlNode of kind yamlNull 
proc newYamlObject(): YamlNode {....raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
Create a new YamlNode of kind yamlObject 
proc newYamlString(s: string): YamlNode {....raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
Create a new YamlNode of kind yamlString 
proc nextToken(p: var YamlParser): YamlToken {....raises: [KeyError, ValueError],
    tags: [], forbids: [].}
Lexical analysis to produce the next token from the input 
proc parseHook(parser: var YamlParser; v: var bool) {.
    ...raises: [KeyError, ValueError], tags: [], forbids: [].}
proc parseHook(parser: var YamlParser; v: var string) {.
    ...raises: [KeyError, ValueError], tags: [], forbids: [].}
proc parseHook[T: enum](parser: var YamlParser; v: var T)
A hook to parse enum fields 
proc parseHook[T: float | float32 | float64](parser: var YamlParser; v: var T)
proc parseHook[T: Integers](parser: var YamlParser; v: var T)
A hook to parse integer fields 
proc parseHook[T](parser: var YamlParser; v: var set[T])
A hook to parse set fields from JSON arrays 
proc parseYAML(input: YAML): YAMLObject {.
    ...raises: [KeyError, ValueError, Exception], tags: [RootEffect], forbids: [].}
proc parseYAML[T: object | ref object](parser: var YamlParser; v: var T)
proc parseYAML[T](input: YAML; t: typedesc[T]): T
Parse YAML string into a Nim object or sequence of type T 
proc put(obj: YAMLObject; key: string; value: YamlNode) {....raises: [], tags: [],
    forbids: [].}
Insert or update a key-value pair in a YAMLObject
